title: Number Systems, Signed Quantities, and Reliable Operations · Model Lens: Place Value
summary: Reconcile inventory gains, losses, temperatures, elevations, and account changes from mixed records. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math beg place value increment in a signed-quantity inventory reconciliation. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use place value, scientific notation, and powers of ten to compare very large and very small quantities.
Classify natural, whole, integer, rational, irrational, and real numbers by the operations a model needs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Calculate Place Value evidence
steps.instruction: A measured quantity starts at 19 units, changes by −6 units, then by +2 units. Calculate the final quantity.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Preserve the signs and compute 19 − 6 + 2; compare with the starting scale.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Preserve the signs and compute 19 − 6 + 2; compare with the starting scale.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes units.
